solution.p: We will factor y^{2}t^{2}-12yt+27 where a=1, b=-12, and c=27.
Mental Method: When we factor a polynomial in the form of x^2+bx+c, x^2+bxy+cy^2, or x^2y^2+bxy+c we can use mental math to factor. We need to find two numbers whose product is c, and whose sum is b.

For y^{2}t^{2}-12yt+27, since the product of -9 and -3 is 27, and the sum of -9 and -3 is -12, the factorization is \begin{aligned}[t]y^{2}t^{2}-12yt+27\amp =\left(yt-9\right)\mathopen{}\left(yt-3\right)\end{aligned}
AC Method: In this method, we first calculate the product of a and c in ax^2+bx+c. For this problem, we have (1) \cdot (27) = 27.

The number 27 can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways:  egin{array}{lll}1(27)\amp -1(-27)\amp 3(9)\amp -3(-9)
ewline\end{array} Looking at the corresponding sums, we want a sum of -12, and this sum happens with (-3)+(-9)=-12 So we write  egin{aligned}[t]y^{2}t^{2}-12yt+27\amp =y^{2}t^{2}-3yt-9yt+27
ewline \amp =yt\mathopen{}\left(yt-3ight)-9\mathopen{}\left(yt-3ight)
ewline \amp =\left(yt-9ight)\mathopen{}\left(yt-3ight)\end{aligned}Generic Rectangles Method: First, we set up generic rectangles by putting the first term and third term in y^{2}t^{2}-12yt+27 into the top left and bottom right rectangles:
The first term's coefficient, 1, can be factored into the product of two numbers (where the first factor is positive) in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(1)\amp \end{array}
The third term, 27, can be factored into the product of two numbers in the following ways: \begin{array}{llll}1(27)\amp -1(-27)\amp 3(9)\amp -3(-9)\newline\end{array}

We put each pair into the corresponding places next to those generic rectangles, and try to match the area of those rectangles with y^{2}t^{2}-12yt+27 by guess-and-check. The following generic rectangles show the solution:
Dimensions of those generic rectangles in the diagram give us the solution: \begin{aligned}[t]y^{2}t^{2}-12yt+27\amp =\left(yt-9\right)\mathopen{}\left(yt-3\right)\end{aligned}
